meaning: adj.濡傜櫨绉戣緸鍏哥殑,鐧剧鍏ㄤ功寮忕殑
examples: He had an encyclopedic knowledge of drugs.
She has an encyclopedic knowledge of natural history.
He had a hard professional competence and an encyclopedic knowledge of his adopted country, an in-depth learning uncorrupted by vague idealism.
America has college museums (Harvard's and Yale's, especially) with encyclopedic riches that equal the world's greatest.
He had an encyclopedic knowledge of commodities, picked up while nosing around the BSE library.
